steps.title: Use the zero rule
steps.content:
For the rational function

$$g(x)=\frac{f(x)}{x-19}$$

an $x$-intercept happens when the numerator is $0$ and the denominator is not $0$.

steps.title: Remove the excluded zero
steps.content:
The function $f$ has $9$ $x$-intercepts total. One occurs at $x=19$, but that value makes the denominator equal to $0$:

$$x-19=0$$

So that zero of $f$ is not an $x$-intercept of $g$.

steps.title: Count the remaining intercepts
steps.content:
The other zeros of $f$ still make the numerator of $g(x)$ equal to $0$ while keeping the denominator nonzero:

$$9-1=8$$

The answer is 8.
